title: Karachi Style Fish Fry

seo.title: Karachi Style Fish Fry Recipe: Authentic Pakistani Flavor
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Karachi Style Fish Fry with our authentic Pakistani recipe! Get crispy, flavorful fish every time. Perfect for seafood lovers. Try it today!

seo.h1: Authentic Karachi Style Fish Fry Recipe
h2Headings: What is Karachi Style Fish Fry?
Ingredients for Karachi Style Fish Fry
How to Make Karachi Style Fish Fry: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Crispy Fish Fry
seo.imageAlt: Platter of golden brown, crispy Karachi Style Fish Fry garnished with lemon wedges and fresh cilantro, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the iconic flavors of Karachi with this irresistible homemade fish fry, a true Pakistani delight!

description: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani seafood dish, known for its incredibly crispy exterior and tender, succulent interior. Each piece of fish is marinated in a vibrant blend of spices, delivering a burst of authentic flavors with every bite. This dish is a true celebration of Karachi's rich culinary heritage, perfect for any occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Wash the fish fillets thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make a few shallow slits on each fillet to help the marinade penetrate deeper. This ensures maximum flavor.

instructions.title: Make the Marinade
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, roasted cumin powder, garam masala, crushed ajwain, and salt. Mix well to form a thick paste. Add a tablespoon or two of water if the paste is too dry.

instructions.title: Marinate the Fish
instructions.text: Generously coat each fish fillet with the prepared spice paste, ensuring it gets into the slits. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-3 hours for best flavor absorption. Overnight marination is even better.

instructions.title: Prepare the Coating Batter
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, whisk together besan (gram flour) and cornflour. Gradually add water, a little at a time, to form a smooth, thick batter that coats the back of a spoon. Ensure there are no lumps.

instructions.title: Coat and Heat Oil
instructions.text: Heat oil in a deep frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. The oil should be hot enough to sizzle when a drop of batter is added, but not smoking. Dip each marinated fish fillet into the besan-cornflour batter, ensuring it is evenly coated on all sides.

instructions.title: Fry the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the coated fish fillets into the hot oil, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Fry for about 4-6 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy, and the fish is cooked through. Turn gently to ensure even frying.

instructions.title: Drain and Garnish
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the fish from the oil using a slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack or paper towels to drain excess oil. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander and lemon wedges. Serve immediately while hot.

tips: For extra crispiness, add a tiny pinch of baking soda to the gram flour mixture.
Always use fresh, firm fish. Kingfish, Snapper, or Pomfret work best for this recipe.
Serve hot immediately with a squeeze of fresh lemon, mint chutney, or tamarind chutney.
article: Karachi Style Fish Fry is a beloved Pakistani Seafood dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
